label: Uncomplicated tinea cruris without cellulitis or immunocompromised host
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated tinea cruris or jock itch.
No cellulitis, abscess, genital ulcer/STI pathway, immunocompromised host, extensive or recurrent fungal rash, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Cellulitis, abscess, pus, fever, systemic illness, severe pain, rapidly progressive rash, or immunocompromised host.
Genital ulcers, STI concern, scrotal/testicular pain, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, extensive or recurrent fungal rash, or specialist-directed dermatology plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care or dermatology if symptoms are not improving, recur often, or spread despite the plan.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus and CDC support tinea-cruris/jock-itch framing as a fungal groin infection, moisture/friction prevention, and antifungal treatment concepts. Autonomous Phase 1751-1800 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated tinea cruris without cellulitis/abscess, genital ulcer or STI pathway, scrotal/testicular emergency concern, immunocompromise, extensive/recurrent disease, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus and CDC support tinea-cruris/jock-itch framing as a fungal groin infection, moisture/friction prevention, and antifungal treatment concepts. Autonomous Phase 1751-1800 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated tinea cruris without cellulitis/abscess, genital ulcer or STI pathway, scrotal/testicular emergency concern, immunocompromise, extensive/recurrent disease, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated tinea cruris or jock itch
no cellulitis, secondary infection, abscess, immunocompromised host, or STI concern documented
no scrotal/testicular, extensive/recurrent, pregnancy, pediatric, or specialist-directed pathway
